mnemonics.title: Chain Rule Melody
mnemonics.content: Derive the new - multiply the rates. For z depends on x and y, x depends on t. dz/dt = dz/dx × dx/dt + dz/dy × dy/dt.

mnemonics.title: Euler's Easy Formula
mnemonics.content: x·fₓ + y·fᵧ = n·f. Think 'xylophone needs n friends' - x-y to the power n.

mnemonics.title: Hessian Test Decision
mnemonics.content: D = fₓₓfᵧᵧ - (fₓᵧ)². D > 0: max/min (check fₓₓ). D < 0: saddle point. D = 0: test inconclusive.

mnemonics.title: Gradient Growth
mnemonics.content: Gradient points UP (steepest ascent). Directional derivative max = |∇f|. Min = -|∇f|.

mnemonics.title: Exact Test
mnemonics.content: Mdx + Ndy exact? Check: ∂M/∂y = ∂N/∂x. Remember: 'My Y at X equals New X at Y'.

acronyms.usage: Used in second derivative test for maxima/minima/saddle points

acronyms.expansion: Nabla/Del operator - vector of partial derivatives
acronyms.usage: ∇f = (fₓ, fᵧ, f_z) gives gradient; ∇² is Laplacian

acronyms.expansion: Equation of Motion - used in wave equation derivation

visualTips.visualization: Imagine cutting the 3D surface z=f(x,y) with a plane y=constant. The slope of the resulting 2D curve is ∂f/∂x.
Draw ∇f as an arrow pointing uphill on a mountain contour map. Arrow length = steepness.
Think of a horse saddle - it curves up in one direction, down in another (like Pringles chip).
Like a perfect square: if Mdx + Ndy is exact, the 'mixed terms' match like puzzle pieces.
Where the 'ball' would balance on the surface - flat in all directions (tangent plane horizontal).

analogies.analogy: Like measuring speed of a car in one lane while ignoring steering (change in other direction).
Like GPS tracking actual speed considering you changed lanes AND accelerated.
Like a compass always pointing uphill - the direction of steepest ascent.
Like a recipe that scales perfectly - doubling ingredients doubles the cake size.
Like a transformation zoom factor - how much area stretches/shrinks when changing coordinates.
